title: Creating a Message Format for a Distributed System
scenario: Design a message format for a fictional distributed system consisting of multiple microservices. The message format should include attributes for request ID, service name, method, input data, and a unique identifier. The format must specify the data types, possible values, and ordering of fields. Use a specific serialization format and ensure the format is self-descriptive and easy to understand for developers.
constraints: Must use a specific serialization format
Must specify the ordering of fields

evaluationCriteria.description: Check if the generated message format is well-formed, valid, and self-descriptive.
Verify that the format includes constraints for valid data types and lengths (e.g., string, integer, array).
Evaluate the format for adherence to distributed system design best practices, including message passing and error handling.
